current_focus.reason: BA-10-S4 closed the downstream supervisor action-catalog gap by adding contact-rooted delayed-feedback follow-through, but the acceptance matrix still holds at 194 implemented / 4 partial / 14 gap scenarios because maintenance automation, chat review/control, idle-timeout resume, and posting-abandon behavior remain open BA-10-S3 hardening work.

gap_summaries.title: Maintenance workflow and artifacts are not implemented

open_scenarios.name: Maintenance change artifacts exist for every autonomous maintenance batch
open_scenarios.rule: Machine handoff contracts and canonical state
open_scenarios.note: Maintenance artifacts are specified in the schema and PRD, but no maintenance batch workflow writes them yet.

open_scenarios.name: Supervisor work selection follows the current default priority order
open_scenarios.note: Current supervisor regressions prove open incidents outrank ordinary pipeline advancement, existing runs outrank new posting bootstrap, new postings outrank opportunistic contact-rooted general-learning work, and contact-rooted general-learning work now covers bounded delayed feedback, send-ready dispatch, and email discovery, but bounded maintenance work itself still has no dedicated selector or action path.

open_scenarios.name: Daily maintenance is mandatory, bounded, and run-boundary aware
open_scenarios.note: Only maintenance placeholders exist today; the maintenance workflow itself is still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Maintenance changes follow the current git and approval workflow
open_scenarios.note: Only maintenance placeholders exist today; the maintenance workflow itself is still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Proper maintenance validation requires both change-scoped and full-project testing
open_scenarios.note: Only maintenance placeholders exist today; the maintenance workflow itself is still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Failed or unapproved maintenance batches remain reviewable
open_scenarios.note: Only maintenance placeholders exist today; the maintenance workflow itself is still missing.

gap_summaries.title: Chat review and control remain wrapper-only

open_scenarios.name: jhc-chat startup dashboard is detailed, bounded, and clean-first
open_scenarios.note: The runtime pack and review-query layer exist, but the direct chat experience has not yet implemented this richer behavior end to end.

open_scenarios.name: Startup dashboard runtime metrics count only active autonomous execution
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Review retrieval is grouped, compact-first, and newest-first within each group
open_scenarios.note: The runtime pack and review-query layer exist, but the direct chat experience has not yet implemented this richer behavior end to end.

open_scenarios.name: jhc-chat uses persisted state for answers and control routing
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Default change summaries cover activity since the last completed expert review
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Expert guidance becomes live immediately but conflicting or uncertain reuse asks first
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Conflicting expert guidance pauses the whole autonomous system
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Expert-requested background tasks require explicit handoff summary and exclusive focus
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Expert-requested background task outcomes return to review appropriately
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: AI agent surfaces the current review queue in chat
open_scenarios.note: The direct `jhc-chat` wrapper manages session state only; chat-side review retrieval, routing, and guidance behaviors are still missing.

open_scenarios.name: Expert-interaction resume follows explicit close, explicit resume, or safe idle timeout
open_scenarios.note: Explicit close and explicit resume paths exist, but automatic idle-timeout recovery after unexpected chat exit is still backlog.

open_scenarios.name: The user may explicitly abandon a posting from any active orchestration state
open_scenarios.note: No posting-abandon runtime control surface or tests exist yet.

commands.title: Runtime pack regressions
commands.description: Confirms generated runtime scaffolding stays honest about current action-catalog and maintenance placeholder status.

commands.title: Acceptance report guards
commands.description: Keeps the committed BA-10 acceptance and blocker reports synchronized with repo code, tests, and state references.

commands.title: Runtime control regressions
commands.description: Covers launchd plist wiring, control commands, chat lifecycle state, delayed feedback runners, and explicit negative control cases.

commands.title: Review surface regressions
commands.description: Verifies persisted grouped review surfaces and traceability reads that back the chat/review boundary.
